rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE JASMINE LIN IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/1/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Has the (former) trustee reviewed prior accountings to confirm that the amounts from December 2004 and on up to this accounting period were not already reimbursed to the parents? It appears that the conservators paid their attorney without an order. [CRC 7.755(a).] No receipt or informational I&A has been filed by the successor co-trustees to confirm the assets reported to have been transferred to them. Is the attorney alleging that his paralegal fully meets the requirements of Business and Professions Code Section 6450? Attorney costs should be fully described. For example, what was sent by courier? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/19/22. No courtesy copy of the proof of service was provided. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VENRICE PALMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/24/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The First Supplement filed 02/07/2022 now alleges the assets on hand on March 31, 2021 is a TD Ameritrade Account #4329 with a balance of $344.52. Even though there has been a waiver of account the Examiner must be able to trace the funds from the I&A value of $1,443,843.54 to the assets on hand of $344.52. Please explain what happened to $1,443,499.02 in estate assets. 2) This issue still hasn't been addressed. On the attorney timesheets please explain why there are the same entries on both the ordinary and extraordinary timesheets. See entries on the extraordinary timesheets for 6/14/2016, 6/15/2016, 06/07/2016, 08/05/2016, and 2 entries on 11/23/2016. It appears the extraordinary request should be reduced by these duplicate entries as they do not appear extraordinary. 3) This issue still hasn't been addressed. The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [LRSF 14.35 I. 6) a.] 4) This issue still hasn't been addressed. The proposed order must state specifically what assets are being distributed only alleging distribute the assets of the estate as follows is not sufficient. 5) This issue still hasn't been addressed. There are no exhibits attached to the filed petition. Please file either a Supplement or Amendment with notice that provides the Exhibits that are described in the petition but not attached. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/9/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEONARDO ROSILLO SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST, CREATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #10, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) The filing of this accounting petition is 2 years late. 2) Attorney requests $25,662 in fees, primarily to: file the 9th accounting, address Examiner's Notes, and to file an amended petition to request investment authority. Attorney hours were charged at $650-$725/hr and paralegal hours at $360/hr. 3) Attorney requests $17,888 for fees related to appealing SSA decision, which is in addition to the $5,076 that has already been paid per court order. 4) $319,500 in additional assets were purchased during this accounting, some of which were new assets for which no support was provided and for which no prior court approval was obtained: a. BNY Mellon Floating Rate Income Fund ($28k, prospective provided) b. Nuance Concentrated Value Fund ($23k, no info provided) c. AMG Managers Picet ($45k, no info provided) d. BBH Select Series 1 ($29k, no info provided) 5) No allegation was made regarding whether the trust has any liabilities. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF AKIKO SPINDLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/17/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1. Please provide a bank statement that verifies the account balance of Chase account #0852 at the end of the account period 11/30/2021. The provided account statement ends 11/08/2021. 2. Please amend the schedule E Non-cash assets to group the assets by account # as some assets do not have account #'s listed. It appears some units numbers do not match the account statements. Some information on Schedule E Non-Cash Assets do not appear to match the provided account statements. 3. All filed timesheets must conform to LRSF 14.52. Time records must identify the billing party providing the service and include the hourly rate for each billing party, the time spent on the services described in the entry, and the total fee charged for that entry. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/2/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE DONALD M. WILSON 2018 LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/31/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The second amended first account showed assets totaling $2,529,487.57 on hand as of 11/30/19. Why does this accounting begin with $2,530,438.62? The date of any order authorizing legal fees must be set forth on the disbursements schedule. Reimbursements to any individual, especially the trustee, must identify the original payee. Courier services should show what "court documents" were sent via courier. What were the dates covered by the payment on 9/24/21 to Lakeside Park? (There were also payments to this facility from the conservatorship estate.) There appear to be several payments to PG&E that were also paid out of the conservatorship estate. See, for example, payments on 5/26/20, 6/26/20, 7/27/20, 8/25/20, and others. Why was there a $254.88 Recology lien paid on 9/24/21? Please file the seller's closing statement to verify the costs of sale. Abandoned motorcycle processing? There was no motorcycle inventoried. There is no liabilities schedule, despite the allegation in paragraph 6 of the petition. Has the overpaid trustee fees been repaid? (The former trustee resigned on 8/27/20; his resignation was filed on 2/9/21 but not made final until the successor was appointed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/19/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHARLES L. JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/1/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No proof of service of the Notice of Hearing. Notice must be given to all of the beneficiaries of the devisee trust if the executor is also the trustee. Original financial account statements (or original letters signed by an officer of the financial institution having the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance) must be filed to verify the ending balance of assets. Any difference between the statement(s) and the account must be reconciled. [LRSF 14.34 D.] The petition fails to disclose whether or not notice was required by, or given pursuant to, Probate Code Sections 9202(b) and (c). The petition must also set forth on what date notice was given to the Franchise Tax Board. The accounting period for a decedent's estate must always begin with the date of death, which is the date on which assets were appraised. Assets on hand at the beginning of the accounting period should be the exact assets appraised on the I&A. If there were receipts of interest or dividends, etc., those should be listed on the schedule of receipts. Why are the assets on hand exactly the same as those on the date of death? Were checks and payments from insurance, etc., not invested into an interest-bearing estate bank account? Is the executor the decedent's daughter? Her initial petition and "supplemental petition" (although there is no such thing) showed her as a sister. Paragraph 24 of the petition must identify the trustee and date of the devisee trust. Who are the beneficiaries of the devisee trust? Nothing appears to have been filed in the proceeding to identify them. (The will was admitted on 8/26/20, but the order was not filed until 8/27/20. The time for filing claims expired on 1/15/21, four months after Letters were issued, not 6/29/20.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/20/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIA DIANE PRICE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Preliminary Distribution [Prob. Code 11620]
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Without prior court approval, the administrator distributed the decedent's two cars exceeding an aggregate value of $50,000 to one of the beneficiaries before the time for filing creditor claims expired. Probate Code section 10520(b). 2) The administrator requests preliminary cash distribution in the total amount of $1,731,000 to the beneficiaries. The Court is to determine whether the requested distribution can be made at this time "without loss to creditors or injury to the estate or any interested person" under section 11621(a) and whether a bond should be required from each beneficiary under section 11622(b). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KAREN RENEE JOLIFFE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar for the petitioner's failure to address the following issues raised by the previous tentative rulings: 1) No original will is on file. 2) No DE-121 form with a proof of service of notice of this petition is on file. 3) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 4) No DE-147S form is on file. 5) No proof of publication is on file. In addition, an amended DE-147 form must be filed to provide the petitioner's personal telephone number instead of his counsel's in the acknowledgment of receipt section. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARK JAMES LYNCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petitioner and John Lynch witnessed the decedent's will. The petitioner and John Lynch are both beneficiaries named in the will. There are no other disinterested witnesses to the will. Probate Code section 6112(c) provides in relevant part that: "Unless there are at least two other subscribing witnesses to the will who are disinterested witnesses, the fact that the will makes a devise to a subscribing witness creates a presumption that the witness procured the devise by duress, menace, fraud, or undue influence. This presumption is a presumption affecting the burden of proof." 2) The petitioner, who resides in the State of Maine, requests that his bond be fixed at $50,000. None of the beneficiaries named in the decedent's will waived the petitioner's bond. The estimated value of the estate property is $200,000. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.
